subject: Geography
title: Sri Lanka: Teardrop of India
description: A short lesson to help you apply Sri Lanka: Teardrop of India.

hook.text: Sri Lanka sits just off India's southern tip, connected by a chain of limestone shoals.

buildup.text: Ancient legend calls this chain 'Adam's Bridge'—it may have been walkable 7,000 years ago.

discovery.text: Sri Lanka is a biodiversity gem with 3,000 plant species in an area smaller than Ireland.

twist.text: Its location on trade routes made it a prize for Portuguese, Dutch, and British empires.

climax.text: The 2004 tsunami killed 30,000 here, exposing the dangers of island coastal geography.

punchline.text: A small island at the crossroads of oceans and empires.

quiz.question: What connects Sri Lanka to India?
options: A chain of limestone shoals called Adam's Bridge
